title: 10 best herbal decoctions for hangover
intro_desc: Herbal decoctions for hangover are natural remedies made by steeping herbs in hot water to create a soothing, caffeine-free beverage that can alleviate the symptoms of a hangover. These decoctions help to replenish lost nutrients, rehydrate the body, and reduce nausea and headache associated with excessive drinking. For example, ginger and peppermint herbal decoctions can ease stomach discomfort and nausea, while chamomile and lavender can promote relaxation and reduce anxiety. By incorporating these herbal decoctions into their recovery routine, individuals can improve their overall well-being, reduce morning-after misery, and start the day feeling refreshed and revitalized.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree decoctions helps with hangover because of its unique combination of bioactive compounds. The decoction contains saponins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids that work synergistically to alleviate symptoms of a hangover. These compounds have been shown to reduce inflammation, oxidative stress, and excitotoxicity in the brain, which are common contributors to hangover symptoms such as headaches, nausea, and fatigue. Additionally, maidenhair tree decoctions have been found to possess anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce the severity of hangover symptoms, making it a natural and effective remedy for those seeking relief from a night out too many.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Ginkgo biloba's antioxidants help reduce oxidative stress caused by excessive alcohol consumption, which can lead to inflammation and damage to liver cells.

Vasodilatory properties: The flavonoids present in Ginkgo biloba decoctions help relax blood vessels, improving circulation and reducing blood pressure, which can alleviate hangover symptoms such as headaches and nausea.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Ginkgo biloba's anti-inflammatory compounds may help reduce inflammation caused by excessive drinking, which can contribute to symptoms like fatigue, irritability, and stomach discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins, which help to neutralize toxins and reduce inflammation associated with hangovers.
Barks: Contain astringent compounds that help to absorb excess fluids and electrolytes lost during heavy drinking.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried ginkgo biloba leaves and add them to a small pot containing 200ml of boiling water.
Reduce heat to low and simmer the mixture for 10-15 minutes to allow the active compounds to infuse into the water.
Strain the decoction through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup, discarding the solids and retaining the liquid.
Add one tablespoon of honey or sugar to the cooled decoction to enhance its palatability and sweetness.
Consume the herbal ginkgo biloba decoction immediately after preparation and drink at least 200ml every hour as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ashwagandha decoctions helps with hangover because it contains adaptogenic compounds that help reduce stress and anxiety caused by excessive alcohol consumption. The anti-inflammatory properties of ashwagandha also help alleviate headaches, fatigue, and nausea commonly associated with hangovers. Additionally, its antioxidant properties may help neutralize free radicals formed during alcohol metabolism, reducing oxidative damage to the body. By promoting relaxation and reducing inflammation, ashwagandha decoctions can provide relief from the discomforts of a hangover, making it a natural remedy worth considering after a night out.
remedy_properties: Adaptogenic Properties: Ashwagandha helps to reduce stress and anxiety caused by hangovers, promoting overall well-being and relaxation.

Antioxidant Properties: Ashwagandha's antioxidants help to neutralize free radicals produced during heavy drinking, reducing oxidative damage to the body and alleviating symptoms like fatigue and inflammation.
GABA-Enhancing Properties: Ashwagandha's GABA-enhancing effects can help to calm the nervous system, reducing hangover-related symptoms such as anxiety, jitteriness, and insomnia.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the ashwagandha plant are rich in bioactive compounds that help alleviate hangover symptoms such as nausea, fatigue, and stress.
Barks: Ashwagandha bark is known for its ability to soothe digestive issues and calm the stomach, making it an effective treatment for hangover-induced gastrointestinal discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Gather dried ashwagandha root powder weighing approximately 2-3 grams.
Boil one cup of water in a saucepan over medium heat for about 8 minutes.
Add the powdered ashwagandha root to the boiling water and stir gently.
Reduce heat to low and simmer the mixture for an additional 10-12 minutes or until it reaches desired strength.
Strain the decoction into a cup and let it cool slightly before consumption within the next 30 minutes of waking up with hangover symptoms.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maypop decoctions helps with hangover because they possess natural antihistamine properties that alleviate symptoms such as headaches, nausea, and dizziness. The plant's active compounds, including flavonoids and alkaloids, work to reduce inflammation and relax muscles, providing relief from hangover-related discomforts. Additionally, maypop's antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties help to neutralize oxidative stress caused by excessive alcohol consumption, promoting a faster recovery and reducing the risk of prolonged hangover symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Maypop contains anti-inflammatory compounds like saponins and flavonoids, which can help reduce inflammation in the body caused by excessive alcohol consumption, alleviating hangover symptoms.
Digestive soothers: The mucilage present in maypop decoctions has a soothing effect on the digestive tract, reducing nausea, bloating, and stomach pain often experienced during a hangover.

Antioxidant properties: Maypop's antioxidant properties can help neutralize free radicals generated by alcohol metabolism, which can reduce oxidative stress and alleviate hangover symptoms like fatigue, headaches, and brain fog.
remedy_parts: Roots: Maypop roots are rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that help alleviate hangover symptoms like headaches and fatigue.
Barks: The barks of the maypop plant contain flavonoids that have been shown to reduce inflammation, making them effective in reducing hangover-related discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried passiflora incarnata flowers, stems, and leaves in quantities of 20-30 grams.
Combine the harvested plant material with 500 milliliters of boiling water in a large pot.
Reduce heat to a simmer for 10-15 minutes allowing the mixture to steep and infuse.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container.
Allow the decoction to cool and store it in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours before consumption.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Highbush cranberry decoctions helps with hangover because of its unique combination of compounds that work synergistically to alleviate symptoms. The decoction's high concentration of antioxidants and flavonoids help to neutralize the damaging effects of ethanol on the body, reducing inflammation and oxidative stress. Additionally, the decoction's anti-inflammatory properties may help to soothe a headache and alleviate nausea, while its natural antibacterial compounds may help to combat the stomach upset often associated with hangovers.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Highbush cranberry decoctions contain anti-inflammatory compounds like quinic acid, which can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress caused by excessive alcohol consumption.

Antioxidants: The decoction is rich in antioxidants, such as anthocyanins and flavonoids, which can help neutralize free radicals produced during hangover and mitigate damage to the liver, kidneys, and other organs.

Digestive-soothing properties: Highbush cranberry has been traditionally used to treat digestive issues, including stomach upset, nausea, and vomiting, common symptoms of a hangover, due to its ability to soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the gut.
remedy_parts: Roots: Known for their anti-inflammatory properties, highbush cranberry roots can help alleviate headaches and nausea caused by excessive drinking.
Barks: The barks of the highbush cranberry tree contain antioxidants that may help counteract the negative effects of alcohol on the liver and kidneys.
remedy_recipe: Measure out one tablespoon of dried vaccinium macrocarpon berries per cup of water.
Combine the measured ingredients in a pot and bring to a boil.
Reduce heat to low and simmer for 20-30 minutes or until the liquid has reduced by half.
Strain the mixture through a fine-mesh sieve into a clean container discarding the solids.
Allow the decoction to cool before refrigerating or freezing for later use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion decoctions helps with hangover because its unique combination of compounds, including inulin, taraxasterol, and sesquiterpenes, work synergistically to counteract the negative effects of excessive alcohol consumption. The decoction's potent diuretic properties help to flush out toxins and excess fluids from the body, alleviating symptoms such as bloating and dehydration. Additionally, dandelion's anti-inflammatory properties may also help to reduce headaches and fatigue associated with hangovers. By replenishing essential nutrients and promoting liver function, dandelion decoctions provide a natural and effective way to alleviate hangover symptoms.
remedy_properties: Diuretic property: Dandelion's diuretic effect helps to flush out excess toxins and waste products from the body, including those accumulated during heavy drinking, which can contribute to hangover symptoms.

Antioxidant property: The antioxidants present in dandelion decoctions help to neutralize free radicals that may have been generated by excessive alcohol consumption, reducing oxidative stress and inflammation that can exacerbate hangover symptoms.

Digestive stimulant property: Dandelion's bitter compounds stimulate digestion and relieve symptoms of nausea, vomiting, and stomach upset commonly associated with hangovers, promoting overall gut health and regular bowel movements.
remedy_parts: Roots: Known for their bitter properties, dandelion roots can help stimulate digestion and relieve nausea associated with hangovers.
Seeds: Rich in vitamins and minerals, dandelion seeds can aid in rehydrating the body after excessive alcohol consumption.
remedy_recipe: Gather fresh taraxacum officinale leaves and stems, measuring one cup of dried plant material.
Boil one liter of water in a pot and add the dried taraxacum officinale, steeping for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a large bowl, discarding solids.
Allow the liquid to cool slightly before dividing it into four equal portions and refrigerating for at least 30 minutes.
Take one portion of the decoction as a natural remedy to alleviate hangover symptoms and promote recovery.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile decoctions helps with hangover because it possesses soothing properties that can alleviate symptoms such as headache, nausea, and dizziness. The apigenin present in chamomile binds to GABA receptors in the brain, promoting relaxation and reducing inflammation. Additionally, chamomile's anti-inflammatory compounds may help calm stomach upset and reduce oxidative stress caused by excessive alcohol consumption. As a result, drinking chamomile decoction can provide gentle relief from hangover symptoms, allowing you to recover more comfortably.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compounds can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation caused by excessive alcohol consumption, which contributes to hangover symptoms like headaches, fatigue, and nausea.

Sedative and anxiolytic properties: Chamomile's sedative and anxiolytic effects can help calm the nervous system, reducing anxiety and stress associated with hangovers, as well as promoting relaxation and improving sleep quality.

Stomach-soothing and anti-nausea effects: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic compounds can help soothe digestive issues like nausea, vomiting, and stomach cramps caused by hangover, allowing for a more comfortable recovery.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of chamomile contain a higher concentration of apigenin, an antioxidant that helps to calm inflammation and soothe digestive issues associated with hangovers.
Flowers: Chamomile flowers are rich in azulene, a compound that promotes relaxation, reduces anxiety, and helps to alleviate headaches and nausea often experienced during or after drinking.
remedy_recipe: Gather one teaspoon of dried matricaria chamomilla flowers per eight ounces of water for the infusion.
Combine the dried flowers and water in a saucepan and bring the mixture to a boil.
Reduce heat to low and simmer the mixture for 5-10 minutes to allow the herbs to steep.
Strain the decoction through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup or teapot and discard solids.
Allow the decoction to cool slightly before consuming as needed to help alleviate hangover symptoms.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger decoctions helps with hangover because its active compounds, such as gingerols and shogaols, have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. These compounds help to alleviate nausea and vomiting, common symptoms of a hangover. Additionally, ginger has natural digestive enzymes that can stimulate the stomach and improve digestion, reducing bloating and discomfort. By drinking an herbal ginger decoction, individuals can quickly replenish lost electrolytes and rehydrate their body, helping to ease the discomfort and fatigue associated with a hangover.
remedy_properties: Digestive stimulant properties: Ginger's digestive stimulant properties help alleviate nausea and vomiting, common symptoms associated with hangovers. This is because ginger increases saliva production, which can help neutralize stomach acid and relieve nausea.

Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties: Ginger's antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties may help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation caused by excessive alcohol consumption, which can contribute to hangover symptoms such as fatigue, headache, and muscle soreness.

Gastric motility-enhancing properties: Ginger's ability to stimulate gastric motility (the movement of food through the digestive system) can help alleviate nausea and vomiting associated with hangovers by speeding up the elimination of toxic substances from the body.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes contain compounds like gingerols and shogaols, which help alleviate nausea and vomiting associated with hangovers.
Roots: Roots have been traditionally used to treat digestive issues and reduce inflammation, making them a valuable component in hangover remedies.
remedy_recipe: Harvest one-inch piece of fresh ginger root weighing about 20 grams.
Peel the ginger root and slice it into thin pieces measuring one-eighth inch thick.
Combine the sliced ginger with four cups of boiling water in a large pot.
Simmer the mixture for 15 to 20 minutes or until the liquid reduces by half.
Strain the decoction through cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a cup.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ivy gourd decoctions helps with hangover because it possesses natural anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help alleviate symptoms such as headache, fatigue, and dizziness. The decoction's diuretic effects also aid in flushing out toxins and excess fluids from the body, reducing dehydration and nausea. Additionally, its antispasmodic properties help ease muscle cramps and digestive discomfort, providing relief from hangover-related misery.
remedy_properties: Diuretic property: Herbal ivy gourd decoction helps to flush out toxins and excess fluids from the body, reducing the severity of hangover symptoms such as nausea, headache, and bloating.

Antioxidant property: The antioxidants present in ivy gourd decoction help to neutralize free radicals that accumulate in the body after excessive alcohol consumption, reducing oxidative stress and inflammation associated with hangovers.

Anti-inflammatory property: Ivy gourd decoction's anti-inflammatory properties may help alleviate headaches, fatigue, and other hangover-related symptoms by reducing swelling and pain caused by inflammatory response.
remedy_parts: Roots: Used to treat nausea and vomiting, roots are a natural anti-inflammatory agent that can help alleviate hangover symptoms.
Rhizomes: Rich in antioxidants, rhizomes can help neutralize free radicals caused by excessive alcohol consumption, reducing inflammation and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 grams of fresh coccinia grandis leaves and rinse them thoroughly under cold running water.
Boil one liter of distilled water in a saucepan for 10 minutes to create a sterilized environment.
Add the harvested coccinia grandis leaves to the boiling water and steep for 20-25 minutes to release active compounds.
Strain the decoction through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a clean glass container and discard solids.
Store the prepared decoction in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours before consumption.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kava decoctions helps with hangover because of its unique combination of bioactive compounds that work together to alleviate symptoms. The roots of the kava plant contain flavonoids, lactones, and phenolic acids, which have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. These compounds help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which are often exacerbated during a hangover. Additionally, kava's sedative and calming effects can help alleviate headaches, nausea, and anxiety, making it a natural remedy to ease the discomfort of a hangover.
remedy_properties: Anxiolytic properties: Kava's anxiolytic effects can help alleviate anxiety and stress symptoms associated with hangovers, promoting relaxation and reducing feelings of panic.
Pain-relieving properties: Kava's ability to reduce pain sensitivity can help ease headaches, body aches, and other physical discomforts commonly experienced during a hangover.
Gastroprotective properties: Kava's gastroprotective effects may help soothe the stomach and alleviate nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea symptoms that often accompany hangovers.
remedy_parts: Roots: Kava roots contain the highest concentration of kavalactones, which help alleviate hangover symptoms by reducing anxiety and promoting relaxation.
Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes, also known as underground stems, have a higher concentration of kavalactones than leaves or other aerial parts, making them ideal for medicinal decoctions to combat hangovers.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried piper methysticum leaves into a heat-resistant glass measuring cup.
Bring one cup of water to a boil in a medium-sized pot and add the measured piper methysticum leaves.
Reduce heat to low and simmer for 15-20 minutes, allowing the mixture to steep and infuse.
Strain the decoction through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a clean glass container, discarding solids.
Allow the decoction to cool and store it in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours before consumption.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tea decoctions helps with hangover because they contain natural compounds that can alleviate symptoms of dehydration, nausea, and fatigue. Herbal teas such as peppermint, ginger, and chamomile have anti-inflammatory properties that soothe the stomach and calm the mind. Decoctions like dandelion root and licorice root help to replenish electrolytes and promote digestion, reducing the discomfort associated with hangover. Additionally, many herbal teas possess antioxidant properties that aid in detoxifying the body after a night of excessive drinking.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Herbal teas like ginger, turmeric, and peppermint contain anti-inflammatory compounds that can help reduce the pain and discomfort associated with hangovers.

Antioxidants: Many herbs used in herbal teas, such as green tea, chamomile, and lemon balm, are rich in antioxidants that can help neutralize the oxidative stress caused by excessive alcohol consumption and alleviate symptoms like fatigue and nausea.
Digestive stimulants: Herbs like peppermint, ginger, and fennel contain natural digestive stimulants that can help ease stomach cramps, bloating, and indigestion associated with hangovers.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, tea roots help alleviate symptoms of hangover such as nausea and headache.
Barks: Containing tannins and flavonoids, tea barks soothe digestive issues and calm the stomach lining, reducing the discomfort caused by excessive drinking.
remedy_recipe: Rinse one teaspoon of dried camellia sinensis leaves in cold running water.
Steep the leaves in boiling water for exactly 10 minutes and strain immediately.
Combine 1/4 teaspoon of dried ginger powder with the tea liquid and stir well.
Allow the mixture to steep for an additional 5 minutes before serving hot.
Consume the entire decoction within 30 minutes after waking up.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat hangover with herbal decoctions is to brew a soothing concoction of peppermint, ginger, and chamomile. Boil 1 tablespoon of dried herbs in 2 cups of water for 5-7 minutes, then strain and drink as a warm tea. This combination helps alleviate nausea, headaches, and stomach upset, promoting a smoother recovery from the previous night's indulgence.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to hangover that are treated with herbal decoctions are various digestive issues, such as food poisoning, indigestion, and stomach upset. Herbal decoctions like peppermint, chamomile, and ginger are commonly used to alleviate symptoms of nausea, vomiting, and bloating. Additionally, decoctions made from licorice root, fennel seeds, and cardamom are also effective in soothing digestive discomforts and reducing inflammation. These herbal remedies can provide natural relief without the side effects associated with over-the-counter medications.
